name: Paperclip Software Factory
description: Thin-fork factory layer for Paperclip that ingests project definition, resolves ambiguity, compiles a Critical DAG, emits task specs, and later governs isolated execution.

phases.name: Interface Lock
phases.description: Lock the planning contract, manifest, and task pack.

phases.name: Intake + Decisions
phases.description: Register artifacts, surface questions, and persist decisions.

phases.name: Compilation
phases.description: Generate the Critical DAG and task specs from clarified inputs.

phases.name: Execution Substrate
phases.description: Provision worktrees, launch tasks, and record execution state.

phases.name: Review + Gates
phases.description: Evaluate gate readiness, review outputs, and block unsafe progression.

phases.name: Recovery + Operator View
phases.description: Resume interrupted work and expose clear operator summaries.

artifacts.title: Factory PRD
artifacts.description: Product definition for the software factory.

artifacts.title: Factory Technical Specification
artifacts.description: Technical contract for the factory planning surface.

artifacts.title: Factory Task Pack
artifacts.description: Execution-grade task pack for FS-00 through FS-07.

questions.text: Should the first persistence slice store factory artifacts by extending the existing document surfaces before adding dedicated project-artifact tables?

decisions.title: Paperclip remains the control plane
decisions.summary: Use Paperclip for operator state, approvals, audit trail, and visibility rather than rebuilding a new control plane.

decisions.title: Critical DAG is the planning model
decisions.summary: Use Critical DAG / CCPM-style decomposition and scheduling to separate shared-state serial work from isolated parallel work.

decisions.title: Product repos stay external
decisions.summary: The Paperclip fork orchestrates external product repositories and must not become a monorepo for generated product code.

gates.title: Interface lock complete
criteria: Shared manifest contract validates the committed sample
Task pack exists for FS-00 through FS-07

gates.title: Clarification + compilation ready
criteria: Decisions can be persisted and superseded
DAG compiler emits a valid manifest
Task specs are generated from clarified inputs

gates.title: Recovery slice ready
criteria: Interrupted runs can be resumed safely
Operator can see open questions and pending gates

tasks.name: Interface lock, architecture, and contract pack
acceptance: Task pack exists for all planned slices

tasks.name: Project artifact registry and intake normalization
acceptance: Factory artifacts can be attached to a project
Intake emits a normalized project record

tasks.name: Question queue and decision log
acceptance: Blocking and non-blocking questions are modeled

tasks.name: Critical DAG compiler and manifest generation
acceptance: Manifest is generated from clarified inputs

tasks.name: Task-spec generator
acceptance: Task specs are rendered from the manifest
Specs include acceptance and finalization discipline
Spec output stays aligned with task metadata

tasks.name: Execution substrate and worktree manager
acceptance: Execution manifests are updated during runs

tasks.name: Recovery and operator summary
acceptance: Operator can see open questions and pending gates
End-to-end bootstrap works on a real project
